steps.title: Contrast editorial stances
steps.content: Harper rejected a unified vision and promoted contributors' autonomy through self-effacement. Burke and Lowe are presented as diverging from that stance, so their decisions must have been more forceful, fixed, or doctrine-driven.

steps.title: Reject nonopposite meanings
steps.content:
• Choice A describes skill, not an editorial stance.
  • Choice C means unclear, but the contrast points toward stronger certainty, not ambiguity.
  • Choice D means modest or plain, which is closer to self-effacing than to its opposite.
The answer is B.
